questions.question: Which Notion plan is required to use Enterprise Search?

options.explanation: Enterprise Search is not available on the Free plan.
This plan does not include the Enterprise Search feature.
The article explicitly states that Enterprise Search is available on the Business or Enterprise Plan.
The 'Team' tier is not mentioned as a specific requirement; Business and Enterprise are.

questions.question: When Enterprise Search uses information from your workspace or connected apps, what does it do?

options.text: It automatically saves the information to your Google Drive
options.explanation: Enterprise Search does not save data to external drives; it simply searches for it.

options.text: It cites its sources so you can go back to the source
options.explanation: The article states that Enterprise Search 'always cite its sources so you can go back to the source'.

options.text: It generates a new page with the summarized information
options.explanation: Enterprise Search provides answers and citations, but does not automatically create new pages.

options.text: It hides the original data to protect privacy
options.explanation: The feature is designed to find and present answers, not to hide source data.

questions.question: How do you limit the scope of your search to only your Notion workspace?

options.explanation: Turning off Web search would still allow the search to look at connected apps.
The article states you can 'Toggle `Apps and integrations` off if you don’t want Enterprise Search to look at your workspace'.
Selecting workspace implies you still have it available, whereas toggling it off restricts access to it.

options.text: Select a specific page in the search bar
options.explanation: This adds context to a query but does not change the default scope of the entire search.

questions.question: Which AI models are available to choose from in Enterprise Search?

options.explanation: While these are available, Google's Gemini is also explicitly mentioned as an option.

options.text: OpenAI's GPT, Anthropic's Claude, and Google's Gemini
options.explanation: The article lists these three specific models as available choices.
The article mentions multiple providers, not just OpenAI.
The article specifies GPT, Claude, and Gemini, not Copilot or Bard.

questions.question: Can Enterprise Search analyze databases, and if so, what level of detail does it support?

options.text: No, databases are ignored by the search
options.explanation: Enterprise Search is capable of analyzing databases.

options.text: Yes, including views, relations, and properties
options.explanation: The article states it can 'look closely at databases — including database views, relations, and properties!'

options.text: Yes, but only if the database is public
options.explanation: Public/private status is not mentioned as a requirement for database analysis.

options.text: Only if you manually select the database first
options.explanation: It can look closely at databases as part of the general search scope.

questions.question: Where do you find the option to change the AI model for your search?

options.explanation: The model selection happens within the Enterprise Search window, not the general settings.

options.text: In the Enterprise Search window in Home
options.explanation: The article instructs you to select 'Auto' in the 'Enterprise Search window in Home' to access the model menu.
Connectors are for data sources, not the model selection.
This menu is unrelated to changing the search model.

questions.question: How can you restrict Enterprise Search from looking at the web?

options.explanation: The article explicitly states you can 'Toggle `Web search` off if you don’t want Enterprise Search to search the web'.
This would restrict it to the web only, not remove web search capability.
There is no uncheck option in 'All sources'; you toggle switches.
This selects the model, not the search scope.

questions.question: Which of the following is a connected app supported by Notion AI Connectors?

options.explanation: Instagram is not listed as a supported integration.
Slack is explicitly listed as one of the apps that can be connected via Notion AI Connectors.
Netflix is not listed as a supported integration.
Amazon is not listed as a supported integration.

questions.question: What is a potential limitation when selecting a specific AI model like GPT?

options.text: It will search the web but ignore your workspace
options.explanation: It might not be able to look at your workspace, but it will likely still search the web.

options.text: It will only look at information from the web
options.explanation: The article notes that 'depending on the model you choose, Notion AI may look only at information from the web'.

options.text: It will be slower than the 'Auto' model
options.explanation: The article does not mention speed differences between models.
Citations remain a feature of Enterprise Search regardless of the model.

questions.question: How can you provide specific pages or people as context to a search query?

options.text: Type their name in the search bar
options.explanation: Simply typing the name is insufficient; the article specifies using the 'Add context' feature or @-mentions.

options.text: Select 'Add context' at the top and select the page or person
options.explanation: The article lists this as the primary method to 'take certain pages or people into account'.

options.text: Create a database entry for them
options.explanation: Creating a database entry is unrelated to adding context for a search query.

options.text: Use the @-mention function in the query
options.explanation: While valid, the text specifically describes 'Add context' as the primary method for selecting specific entities.
